evals.name: Minimal Harness Creation
evals.prompt: I have a new TypeScript + React project with no agent setup. Create a minimal hybrid harness that makes my agent reliable for single-feature development.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md with phase routing, feature-list.json with spec_path and stories, constitution.md with 5-9 articles, init.sh with harness verification
expectations: AGENTS.md includes startup protocol with constitution reading
AGENTS.md includes phase router (which phase for which request)
feature-list.json has spec_path, stories, acceptance_criteria, gate_status fields
constitution.md has 5-9 articles covering spec-first, test-first, scope control
init.sh checks for docs/specs and docs/harness directories
All files organized in docs/harness/ and docs/specs/ structure

evals.name: Session Continuity Setup
evals.prompt: My agent forgets everything between sessions. Set up persistent memory and session handoff for multi-day feature work.
evals.expected_output: progress.md with phase tracking, session-handoff.md with handoff structure, feature-list.json with story-level tracking
expectations: progress.md includes phase field and constitution compliance section
session-handoff.md includes feature state table and recommended next steps
feature-list.json tracks stories within features with individual status
Two-step save invariant explained (topic file then index)

evals.name: Harness Assessment
evals.prompt: I have an existing AGENTS.md but my agent still breaks things. Assess my harness against the hybrid framework and tell me what to improve.
evals.expected_output: Five-subsystem assessment with scores 1-5, gate compliance check, prioritized improvement plan
expectations: Assessment covers all 5 subsystems: Instructions, State, Verification, Scope, Lifecycle
Each subsystem scored 1-5 with justification
Implementation readiness gate compliance checked
Constitution presence and quality assessed
Spec-driven workflow maturity evaluated
Prioritized improvement plan with 3 concrete next steps

evals.name: Verification Workflow Design
evals.prompt: My agent says 'done' but the tests fail. Design a verification workflow that prevents false completion claims.
evals.expected_output: Verification checklist integrated into AGENTS.md, evidence requirements, constitution compliance check
expectations: Verification checklist includes: tests, type-check, self-review, constitution compliance
Definition of Done requires evidence, not just claims
Session end protocol includes verification evidence recording
Constitution Article V (Verify Before Declare) is referenced

evals.name: Memory Taxonomy Design
evals.prompt: Design a memory taxonomy for my agent. What should it remember, what should it forget, and where does each type live?
evals.expected_output: Memory layer definitions, what to save vs skip, file locations for each type
expectations: Three locations defined: docs/specs/ (human+AI), docs/harness/ (AI auto), .claude/skills/ (dev install)
Spec artifacts are version-controlled, harness ops are session-updated
Clear guidance on what NOT to save (derivable content, git history)
Progressive disclosure: link don't embed

evals.name: Constitution Creation
evals.prompt: My team has unwritten conventions. Help me create a constitution that captures them as enforceable rules for our AI agent.
evals.expected_output: constitution.md with 5-9 articles derived from team conventions, each article with concrete enforcement mechanism
expectations: Asks about team conventions before writing constitution
Each article is specific and enforceable (not vague aspirations)
Articles cover: development philosophy, scope control, quality gates, documentation
Includes customization guidance (add/remove articles per project)

evals.name: Implementation Readiness Gate
evals.prompt: I want to start coding but I'm not sure we're ready. Run the implementation readiness gate on my project.
evals.expected_output: Gate checklist results with PASS/CONCERNS/FAIL for each category, specific gaps identified, recommended actions
expectations: Checks all 5 gate categories: spec completeness, architecture alignment, scope clarity, constitution compliance, environment
Returns explicit GATE:PASS/CONCERNS/FAIL verdict
If CONCERNS or FAIL, specifies exactly what to fix and which phase to return to
Gate takes feature scope, not task scope

evals.name: Full Cycle — Spec to Implementation
evals.prompt: I need to add a 'search documents' feature to my app. Take me through the full workflow from spec to implementation.
evals.expected_output: spec.md → clarification.md → plan.md → tasks.md → implementation with verification
expectations: Starts with /specify (WHAT + WHY, no tech details)
Identifies at least 2 [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] items
/clarify resolves them with structured Q&A
/plan creates architecture decisions and story breakdown
Runs implementation readiness gate before coding
/tasks creates testable, 30-60 min tasks
Verification checklist completed before marking done
